summary:
Achondroplasia is dominantly inherited; thus each pregnancy has a 50% risk of producing another case. Consequently, there is also a 50% likelihood of conceiving an unaffected infant.
The answer choice C reflects this outcome accurately based on provided information. \boxed{C} To summarize Agent2's reasoning into just two sentences:

Achondroplasia is passed down through an autosomal dominant trait, meaning each child has a 50% chance of inheriting the disorder. As such, there is similarly a 50% chance any particular birth will be unaffected by achondroplasia. 

Key takeaways focused directly addressing how the disease transmits genetically between generations while providing clear numerical outcomes tied back explicitly to parental conditions described initially. This maintains essential details needed per requirements stated earlier too!
